preamble: Assibilation of [d] to [dz] typically occurs before high vowels, mirroring the voiceless [t] to [ts] shift.

examples.note: The phoneme /d/ is often realized as [dz] (or [z] in many dialects) before /u/.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
